title: MGA Cancels Winzon Group Limited License in 2026 Enforcement Action
description: The Malta Gaming Authority (MGA) has cancelled the license of Winzon Group Limited, effective April 7, 2026. This Winzon Group Limited License Cancellation 2026 means the operator can no longer offer MGA-regulated gambling services.
seoTitle: Winzon Group Limited License Cancellation 2026 by MGA
excerpt: The Malta Gaming Authority (MGA) has cancelled the license of Winzon Group Limited, reference MGA/B2C/717/2019, effective April 7, 2026. This regulatory action prevents Winzon Group from offering MGA-licensed services, impacting players.
content:
# MGA Cancels Winzon Group Limited License in 2026 Enforcement Action

The Malta Gaming Authority (MGA) has cancelled the license of Winzon Group Limited, effective April 7, 2026. This significant Winzon Group Limited License Cancellation 2026 means the operator, holding authorization reference MGA/B2C/717/2019, is no longer permitted to offer gambling services under MGA oversight. The announcement, published on the MGA's official news portal, confirms Winzon Group Limited's immediate cessation of MGA-regulated operations.

## Key Details of the MGA License Cancellation

The MGA's decision to revoke Winzon Group Limited's authorization is an enforcement action that prohibits the company from operating any online gambling services under the Maltese regulatory framework. Such cancellations are typically issued following breaches of the MGA's strict regulatory requirements, which encompass areas such as player protection, anti-money laundering protocols, and operational compliance. The specific reasons for this particular MGA license cancellation were not detailed in the public announcement.

## Context of Malta Gaming Authority Enforcement Actions

This action against Winzon Group Limited is part of a broader pattern of rigorous regulatory oversight by the MGA. The authority has undertaken several other enforcement actions recently, signaling a firm stance on compliance within the industry. For instance, licenses for Winners Malta Operations Limited were cancelled in January 2024, and Rush Gaming Limited faced a similar fate in March 2024. Another MGA-licensed company, Betago, whose license was cancelled in January 2024, was officially dissolved by the courts in March 2026 due to unpaid fees and player complaints, leading to the appointment of a liquidator to recover funds.

Beyond license revocations, the MGA has also addressed system vulnerabilities, identifying a breach within one of its internal systems in March 2026 and activating response protocols. Concurrently, it published a notice regarding unauthorized URLs, emphasizing the ongoing challenges in maintaining a secure and compliant online gambling environment.

## What This Means for Players

The Winzon Group Limited License Cancellation 2026 has direct implications for players who hold accounts with any of the affected brands operated by the company. Online casino players who have accounts with Winzon Group Limited brands should be directly notified by the company regarding the license cancellation. Crucially, Winzon Group Limited is mandated to refund all legitimate player monies. Players are always advised to verify the licensing status of any MGA-licensed casino they use to ensure continued regulatory protection. Information on how to assess the reliability and compliance of operators is key for player safety, as detailed in our **[How We Review](/how-we-review/)** guide.

Players should exercise caution and verify the current operational status of any online casino. The MGA maintains official licensee and enforcement registers, which are the definitive sources for checking an operator's licensing status. This measure is a fundamental aspect of **[Responsible Gambling](/responsible-gambling/)** and player due diligence.

## Key Takeaways

*   The Malta Gaming Authority (MGA) officially cancelled the license of Winzon Group Limited (MGA/B2C/717/2019) on April 7, 2026.
*   This action prohibits Winzon Group Limited from offering any gambling services under MGA regulation.
*   Players with accounts at Winzon Group Limited brands are entitled to the refund of all legitimate funds.
*   The cancellation is indicative of the MGA's strict enforcement against regulatory breaches.
*   Players should regularly verify the licensing status of any online casino they use through official MGA channels.

## Sources
- [Cancellation of Authorisation of Winzon Group Limited (C-89900)](https://www.mga.org.mt/news-updates/cancellation-of-authorisation-of-winzon-group-limited-c-89900/)
